prompt: Create a museum-grade brand mark for TheWorkshop, an open-source execution framework for coding agents. The symbol should communicate orchestration, layered workflow, durable knowledge, and calm technical authority, and it should read instantly at favicon scale.
scene: transparent studio backdrop with no visible environment
subject: a single geometric emblem that suggests stacked work surfaces and a guiding orbit or wayfinding ring without turning into a generic gear or swoosh
style: editorial identity mark, vector-friendly flat illustration, elegant and deliberate, more like an enamel badge or wayfinding symbol than an app-store icon
composition: single centered emblem with strong silhouette, quiet margin, and crisp edge separation for docs favicon and social preview use
lighting: minimal soft depth only where it helps silhouette clarity, no dramatic glow
palette: deep navy, oxidized teal, warm brass, parchment cream
materials: screenprinted ink, matte enamel, cut-paper geometry
constraints: no text, no initials, no wrench, no hammer, no mascot, no photoreal scene, transparent background, strong legibility at small sizes
negative: clip-art, glossy app icon treatment, chrome, bevels, random gradients, startup blobs, purple neon, sci-fi logo cliché

prompt: Create a wide systems atlas poster for TheWorkshop with three explicitly separated, connected lanes labeled Planning & Policy, Native Subagent Execution, and Observability & Staged Learning. Each lane should contain a few monumental modules with large sparse labels: Planning Cards, Execution Desks, Signal Routes, Staged Memory Proposals, Curator Checkpoints, Staged Lesson Candidates, and Closeout Gate. The result should feel like an editorial foldout about systems design rather than a software marketing diagram.
scene: warm archival paper background with restrained texture and generous breathing room
subject: a composed architecture poster with strong three-lane structure, elegant routing arrows, and bold modules for planning, execution, telemetry, staged learning, curator control, and explicit closeout
style: high-end editorial systems illustration, bauhaus-meets-information-design, tactile and architectural rather than generic SaaS
composition: wide panoramic poster, clear left-to-right flow, three visible lanes, monumental modules, strong negative space, suitable for a README hero image
lighting: soft studio light with subtle shadows that separate planes without adding clutter
palette: parchment, deep navy, teal, rust, brass, charcoal
materials: paper panels, ink rules, glass overlays, matte technical surfaces
constraints: only a few large readable labels, no tiny UI text, no code snippets, no stock people, no purple theme, no dark mode, no cluttered dashboards
negative: generic dashboard screenshot, cyberpunk neon, childish infographic, clip-art arrows, isometric cityscape, startup illustration

prompt: Create a polished promo visual for an interactive open-source explainer about native subagents in TheWorkshop. Show a crafted browser-style knowledge canvas with large, readable section labels focused on TheWorkshop concepts: Decision Filter, Explorer, Worker, Reviewer, Execution Paths, Staged Learning, and Manual Closeout. In the execution-path row, use Workshop-style path labels such as Manual Path, Dispatch Path, and Loop Path rather than generic placeholders. The image should feel like a premium documentation feature and should look clearly tied to a real systems workflow, not a generic product ad.
scene: light editorial workspace atmosphere with warm paper tones and clean depth
subject: a browser-window style preview of a subagents explainer page with structured sections for Decision Filter, TheWorkshop Role Desk, Execution Paths, Staged Learning, and explicit closeout
style: art-directed documentation mockup, premium UI illustration, tactile and intentional
composition: wide preview image with the mock browser slightly angled or elevated, strong focal hierarchy, enough margin for GitHub markdown display
lighting: soft daylight with subtle shadowing and warm highlights
palette: paper cream, navy, teal, rust, brass
materials: glass UI cards, paper texture, matte interface surfaces
constraints: use the exact large labels Decision Filter, Explorer, Worker, Reviewer, Execution Paths, Staged Learning, and Manual Closeout; use Manual Path, Dispatch Path, and Loop Path in the execution-path row; avoid tiny body text, no lorem ipsum, no watermarks, no fake unrelated roles, no generic action phrases like explore options or execute a task, no Outcome A or Outcome B labels
negative: template SaaS hero, purple gradients, glossy 3D bubbles, clutter, stock-photo office scene, generic hackathon UI, bland slide deck
